seoTitle: How to Rename a Microsoft Loop Workspace (2026)
metaDescription: Update a Microsoft Loop workspace's title and icon from the home dashboard, and learn which default workspaces can't be renamed.
intro: Outdated or confusing workspace names make it harder to find the right project directory. This guide covers renaming a Microsoft Loop workspace and updating its cover icon from the home dashboard, plus what to do if you run into a workspace that won't let you rename it.

steps.heading: Return to the home dashboard
steps.body: Go to loop.cloud.microsoft and click the top left Loop logo to access your master workspaces board.

steps.heading: Find the workspace to rename
steps.body: Move your cursor to the specific workspace tile or row item you want to rebrand.

steps.heading: Open the rename options
steps.body: Hover over the title, click the three dots icon, and select 'Rename and style' from the dropdown menu.

steps.heading: Update the name and icon
steps.body: Highlight the text field, type your new descriptive name, and update the header emoji if you want a new visual identity.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Click the Update button to save the new title and push it to all connected team views.

faq.question: Can I rename any workspace in Loop?
faq.answer: Most workspaces can be renamed, but system-locked personal folders like 'My Workspace' may restrict custom title changes.

faq.question: Will renaming a workspace break existing links to it?
faq.answer: Renaming a workspace's display title generally doesn't break the underlying link structure, but it's still good practice to verify shared links after a rename.

faq.question: Does renaming a workspace update it for everyone right away?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking Update pushes the new title instantly across all connected team views.

faq.question: Can I change the icon without changing the name?
faq.answer: Yes, the rename and style panel lets you update the title and the icon independently.
